next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 7

The road improved before the town appeared. For six days Kem had been walking on dirt tracks, game trails, whatever path Vel chose that kept them below ridgelines and out of sight. Now the ground under his boots was packed and graded — not cobblestone, nothing the Vault would recognize as a proper surface, but someone had used a Shaping ability on this stretch of earth. The surface was compressed and even, rainwater channels cut along both sides. Maintained. A road built by spending.

Vel didn't comment on it. She adjusted her pack and kept walking, her pace the same efficient tempo she held whether the ground was flat or climbing, and Kem had stopped trying to match it and settled into his own stride, which was longer but less economical. His blisters had hardened into calluses over the last four days. That counted as progress.

The settlement came into view as they crested a long rise: a cluster of buildings in a shallow valley, sixty or seventy structures, with fields spreading out on three sides and a line of low hills to the south. Early summer light sat heavy on the rooftops, and the heat had a different quality here than in the forested highlands — drier, closer, the kind of warmth that pressed against exposed skin and stayed. It was the largest thing Kem had seen since the Vault, and it looked nothing like the Vault. The buildings were stone and timber, shaped but not uniform — some walls curved where a Shaping ability had been applied freehand, others squared off with hand-laid block. Repairs showed as visible seams between ability-work and manual labo...
open_hooks_to_carry: Sable's bureaucratic competence and field incompetence
The team of untrained level-300 agents — institutional absurdity at peak
Sable's first ability use and its complex emotional aftermath
The Red Reserve named and rejected as disproportionate — for now

payoffs_to_land.description: The Red Reserve is explicitly named and its nature clarified — a secret unit of Hoarded agents trained and permitted to spend for security purposes

prev_tail_excerpt:
...im recalculating something — not the Ledger arithmetic but the simpler math of who she was. The seventeen-year-old who'd jumped into a canal. The fifty-eight-year-old who'd been carrying the heavy pack and hadn't mentioned it. The person between those two points who'd spent two hundred and seventy-two levels, one at a time, and was still walking.

"Thirty-five pounds," he said. "Yeah. Okay. I'll carry it."

Vel banked the coals. She laid out the bedrolls — his close enough to the fire's residual warmth, hers between him and the trail, habit more than any specific threat. The highlands were quiet. No pursuit. The Hoarded would reorganize, send more people, but not tonight. Tonight the world was the size of a campfire: a stream, two bedrolls, the smell of burned grouse and woodsmoke, and the weight of what they'd said to each other sitting between them like something solid they'd both have to carry from here.

She lay on her back. Through the canopy she could see a few stars in the gaps between leaves, small and far away. The stream sounded like the stream and nothing else. A good sound. One of the free ones.

Twenty-eight. The Ledger's hum was there, the way it was always there — not loud, just present, the awareness of what remained. Two hundred and seventy-two levels spent. Twenty-eight left. Every one of the spent ones had gone to something. Someone. Some moment where the water was rising and nobody was moving and she was standing there with what she had.

Twenty-eight was tight. Twenty-eight was enough.

She closed her eyes. In the morning she'd make him do it all again.
secondary_character_beats: Lady Aldraste's political pressure felt through Council framing — retrieving Kem is institutional necessity, not parental rescue

setups_to_plant.description: The Council refuses to deploy the Red Reserve for a retrieval, framing it as an admission that inaction philosophy has failed — establishes trained spenders as an escalation the Hoarded are reluctant to authorize

state_deltas_required: Institutional absurdity meeting real-world demands; Sable's first encounter with action
The pursuit is organized; Sable begins her transformation; the Red Reserve is seeded as latent threat
